proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_080 (range 30-90, mean 65) has a strong positive correlation with pfs_months, consistent with a dominant prognostic continuous covariate (e.g., age-like or hormone-receptor-quantitative variable).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson correlation of feature_080 with pfs_months is r=+0.7015 (p<1e-300). Each 1-unit increase in feature_080 is associated with ~0.176 mo additional PFS in OLS. Strongest single predictor in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 3-level ordinal feature_063 (values 0/1/2 with frequencies 17,592 / 24,971 / 7,437) is a stage-like prognostic variable: higher levels are associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotone fashion.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs by feature_063: 0->5.64, 1->4.44, 2->3.29 mo. Spearman rho = -0.334 (p<1e-300). Strong monotone negative ordinal effect; magnitude ~ -1.2 mo per level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_056 (binary, prevalence 0.30) is associated with shorter pfs_months in unadjusted analysis (negative prognostic factor or risky exposure).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 3.61 with feature_056=1 (n=14,969) vs 5.15 without (n=35,031). Welch t-test p<1e-300. Difference -1.54 months — largest binary main effect in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 (binary, prevalence 0.35) is associated with longer pfs_months in unadjusted analysis (candidate beneficial treatment or favorable biomarker).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 5.41 with feature_042=1 (n=17,428) vs 4.31 without (n=32,572). p<1e-300. Difference +1.10 months — largest positive binary main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_048 (binary, prevalence 0.10) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative prognostic).
feature_111 (binary, prevalence 0.70) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive prognostic / candidate hormone-receptor-positive marker).
feature_015 (binary, prevalence 0.35) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_040 (binary, prevalence 0.18) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_039 (binary, prevalence 0.64) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs: 3.74 with feature_048=1 (n=5,090) vs 4.79 without. p=1.45e-178. Difference -1.05 mo.
Mean pfs: 4.86 with feature_111=1 (n=34,886) vs 4.30 without. p=3.7e-143. Difference +0.57 mo.
Mean pfs: 4.31 with feature_015=1 (n=17,365) vs 4.87 without. p=6.07e-144. Difference -0.56 mo.
Mean pfs: 4.32 with feature_040=1 (n=9,060) vs 4.78 without. p=6.10e-70. Difference -0.46 mo.
Mean pfs: 4.83 with feature_039=1 (n=31,954) vs 4.46 without. p=5.82e-59. Difference +0.36 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous (likely lab/vital) covariates, feature_067 has a negative correlation with pfs_months, while feature_019 has a positive correlation, and feature_101 has a negative correlation.

analyses.result_summary: feature_067: r=-0.116 (p=4.07e-148); feature_019: r=+0.100 (p=9.39e-112); feature_101: r=-0.091 (p=2.98e-93). All three confirm secondary continuous prognostic role beyond feature_080.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The remaining ordinal features (feature_021, feature_024, feature_016, feature_127, feature_001, feature_073) have no meaningful univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho values: f021=+0.005 (p=0.22), f024=-0.001 (p=0.78), f016=-0.0000 (p=0.997), f127=+0.003 (p=0.46), f001=-0.004 (p=0.41), f073=+0.003 (p=0.57). None significant; only feature_063 carries ordinal prognostic information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_011) is associated with mean pfs_months, with white patients having different mean PFS than non-white patients (testing for crude disparity).
Insurance (feature_089) is associated with mean pfs_months (testing for crude disparity by payer).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs by race: white 4.679, asian 4.736, black 4.717, hispanic 4.707, other 4.762. ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.494. No crude disparity in PFS by race.
Mean pfs by insurance: medicaid 4.706, medicare 4.692, private 4.685, uninsured 4.733. ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.818. No crude disparity in PFS by payer.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_080 and feature_063, the binary main effects of feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015, and feature_040 remain large and significant; weaker univariate signals (feature_039, feature_034, feature_068, feature_028, feature_020) attenuate to nonsignificance.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_111 + feature_015 + feature_039 + feature_040 + feature_034 + feature_068 + feature_028 + feature_020', df).fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a strong positive interaction between feature_042 and feature_111 on pfs_months: the favorable effect of feature_042 is concentrated among feature_111=1 patients (consistent with a predictive biomarker pattern).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_042:feature_111 coefficient = +1.58 mo (p<1e-300). Stratified: feature_042 effect within feature_111=1 is +1.58 mo (p<1e-300, n=34,886) vs -0.02 mo (p=0.63) within feature_111=0. Strong predictive biomarker pattern: feature_042 only helps when feature_111=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 abrogates the favorable effect of feature_042: there is a strong negative feature_042 x feature_015 interaction such that feature_042 has no effect when feature_015=1.

analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_015 coefficient = -1.67 mo (p<1e-300). Stratified: feature_042 effect within feature_015=0 is +1.66 mo (p<1e-300, n=32,635) vs +0.04 mo (p=0.29) within feature_015=1. Confirms feature_015 nullifies feature_042 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_040 also abrogates the favorable effect of feature_042: there is a strong negative feature_042 x feature_040 interaction.

analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_040 coefficient = -1.38 mo (p<1e-300). Stratified: feature_042 effect within feature_040=0 is +1.35 mo (p<1e-300, n=40,940) vs -0.01 mo (p=0.77) within feature_040=1. Confirms feature_040 also nullifies feature_042 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining a composite 'biomarker_eligible' indicator as feature_111=1 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_040=0 (37.4% of cohort, n=18,681) maximally separates feature_042 responders from non-responders: among biomarker_eligible patients, feature_042 yields a ~+2.9 month PFS gain vs no effect in biomarker-ineligible.

analyses.result_summary: Biomarker_eligible=1 (n=18,681): pfs 7.21 with feature_042=1 (n=6,571) vs 4.30 without (n=12,110); diff=+2.91 mo (p<1e-300). Biomarker_eligible=0 (n=31,319): pfs 4.32 with feature_042 vs 4.32 without; diff=+0.003 mo (p=0.91). Treatment-biomarker interaction is essentially perfect: feature_042 helps only the biomarker-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-positive subgroup, the magnitude of feature_042's benefit is homogeneous across feature_063 stage levels (i.e., the relative benefit of treatment does not depend on disease stage).

analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker_eligible=1: feature_042 effect by feature_063: stage 0 +2.92 mo (p=1e-284, n=6,575); stage 1 +2.92 mo (p<1e-300, n=9,371); stage 2 +2.97 mo (p=5.9e-136, n=2,735). Effect magnitude is essentially constant across stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-positive subgroup, feature_042's PFS benefit is also homogeneous across feature_080 quartiles (no treatment-effect modification by age-like covariate).

analyses.code: df['ageq']=pd.qcut(df.loc[df['bm']==1,'feature_080'],4); for q in quartiles: stratified t-test
analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker_eligible=1: feature_042 effect by feature_080 quartile: Q1 +2.98 mo, Q2 +2.97 mo, Q3 +2.80 mo, Q4 +2.86 mo (all p<1e-230). Treatment benefit roughly constant across the feature_080 distribution.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Unlike feature_042, the negative-prognostic features feature_056 and feature_048 have UNIFORM effects across the candidate biomarker subgroups — they do not interact with feature_111, feature_015, or feature_040, suggesting they are not predictive biomarkers but rather generalized adverse prognostic factors (e.g., comorbidity or visceral metastasis).

analyses.code: for bm_col in ['feature_111','feature_015','feature_040','feature_063']: stratified ttest of feature_056 and feature_048 effect on pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: feature_056 effect by feature_111: -1.53/-1.54 mo; by feature_015: -1.54/-1.53 mo; by feature_040: -1.53/-1.58 mo; by feature_063: -1.60/-1.51/-1.48 mo (all p<1e-150). Same uniformity for feature_048 (-1.0 to -1.1 mo across all strata). No predictive interaction; consistent with stable negative prognostic factors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_011) does not modify the favorable effect of feature_042 within the biomarker-positive subgroup — i.e., no racial disparity in benefit from the targeted treatment.

analyses.code: for r in races: ttest of feature_042 effect within feature_011=r and biomarker_eligible=1
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect within biomarker-positive subgroup, by race: white +2.89 mo (n=12,203), asian +2.98 mo (n=1,107), black +2.90 mo (n=2,223), hispanic +2.96 mo (n=2,796), other +2.87 mo (n=352). All p<1e-15. Effect magnitude virtually identical across races; no disparity in treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance (feature_089) does not modify the favorable effect of feature_042 within the biomarker-positive subgroup — i.e., no payer-level disparity in benefit from treatment.

analyses.code: for ins in payers: ttest of feature_042 effect within feature_089=ins and biomarker_eligible=1
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect within biomarker-positive subgroup, by insurance: medicaid +3.02 mo (n=2,603), private +2.93 mo (n=7,504), medicare +2.86 mo (n=7,804), uninsured +2.77 mo (n=770). All p<1e-31. Effect magnitudes overlap; no payer-level disparity in treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt rates of feature_042 (i.e., rates of treatment assignment) within the biomarker-positive subgroup are similar across racial and insurance categories — no access disparity.

analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker-positive subgroup, feature_042 receipt rates: white 35.3%, asian 34.6%, black 34.3%, hispanic 35.7%, other 36.1% (chi-square chi2=1.39, p=0.85). By insurance: medicaid 34.3%, medicare 35.6%, private 34.7%, uninsured 37.8% (chi2=4.52, p=0.21). No statistically significant disparity in treatment receipt by race or insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stage at presentation (feature_063) is also distributed equally across racial and insurance categories, indicating no upstream disparity in disease-extent at cohort entry.

analyses.result_summary: Stage proportions are nearly identical across races (white: 35/50/15%, black: 35/50/15%, hispanic: 35/50/15%, asian: 36/48/15%, other: 36/50/15%; chi2=3.59, p=0.89) and across payers (chi2=3.90, p=0.69). No upstream stage disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 has a strong, monotone, approximately linear association with pfs_months — moving from the lowest to the highest quintile shifts mean PFS from ~2.3 to ~7.2 months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs by feature_080 quintile: Q0 2.28, Q1 3.71, Q2 4.67, Q3 5.64, Q4 7.21 mo. Monotone, approximately linear; consistent with the OLS slope of +0.176 mo per unit. feature_080 alone explains ~49% of pfs_months variance (r^2 = 0.7015^2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067, feature_019, and feature_101 retain independent prognostic significance after adjustment for feature_080, feature_063, and the strong binary effects (feature_056, feature_048, feature_042 x biomarker).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_042*biomarker_eligible + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (mo): feature_067 -0.077 per unit (p<1e-300), feature_019 +0.482 per unit (p<1e-300), feature_101 -0.019 per unit (p<1e-300). All three remain strongly significant after adjustment. Quartile means confirm direction (feature_067 Q4 mean 4.29 vs Q1 4.98; feature_019 Q4 4.98 vs Q1 4.39; feature_101 Q4 4.31 vs Q1 4.94).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After fully adjusting for feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101, and feature_042 x biomarker_eligible, race (feature_011) and insurance (feature_089) have no residual association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_111 + feature_015 + feature_040 + feature_042 + feature_042:biomarker_eligible + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101 + C(feature_011) + C(feature_089)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Race coefficients (vs reference 'asian'): black -0.016 (p=0.41), hispanic -0.015 (p=0.42), other -0.020 (p=0.52), white -0.026 (p=0.12). Insurance coefficients (vs medicaid): medicare -0.010 (p=0.41), private -0.010 (p=0.44), uninsured -0.032 (p=0.15). All NSS. Confirms equity of outcomes by demographics after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model containing feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101, and the feature_042 x biomarker_eligible interaction explains the great majority of pfs_months variance, with predictive biomarker structure dominating treatment effect.

analyses.code: Final model (see iteration 23) with R^2 reporting
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.876, n=50,000. Largest coefficients: feature_080 +0.176 mo/unit (t=445), feature_063=2 -2.34 mo (t=-192), feature_063=1 -1.19 mo (t=-137), feature_056 -1.55 mo (t=-181), feature_042:biomarker_eligible +2.91 mo (t=184), feature_048 -0.97 mo (t=-74), feature_019 +0.48 mo/unit (t=61), feature_067 -0.08 mo/unit (t=-75), feature_101 -0.019 mo/unit (t=-58). feature_042 main effect alone is essentially zero (+0.004, p=0.71) — entire treatment benefit is captured by interaction with biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No additional binary feature outside the identified set (feature_042, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_056, feature_048) modifies the feature_042 treatment effect after accounting for the established biomarker; remaining low-magnitude binary main effects (e.g., feature_034, feature_077, feature_086) reflect minor residual prognostic signal rather than predictive interactions.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_042*'+c, df).fit().pvalues['feature_042:'+c]
analyses.result_summary: Scanning all 81 other binary features for interaction with feature_042 (adjusting for feature_080 and feature_063), only feature_111 (+1.58, p<1e-300), feature_040 (-1.38, p<1e-300), feature_015 (-1.67, p<1e-300), and feature_039 (+1.05, p~1e-286, largely collinear with feature_111) emerge as substantial. The next strongest, feature_034 (+0.40, p=2.8e-42), is roughly four-fold smaller and likely reflects partial overlap with the biomarker. No additional independent predictive biomarker is detected.
